item.title: Crimson Desert: How to Get the Darkbringer Sword
item.description:
Darkbringer in Crimson Desert is one of the strongest two-handed swords that can quickly wipe out hordes of enemies with its unique ability. On top of being a tier four weapon with great DPS stats, the weapon comes with Ator's Orb ability, which sends out projectile orbs that track and damage enemies. If you're looking for a weapon to take on high-difficulty liberation missions that pit you against swarms of enemies, Darkbringer is the weapon for you.

Darkbringer Location in Crimson Desert

To get the Darkbringer sword in Crimson Desert , players will need to travel to the White Wastes Sanctuary , located northwest of the village of Pailune. The area is covered in snow and will inflict Ice status on Kliff, so make sure to wear equipment with Ice Protection and carry food that offers temporary boosts against frost.

Once you arrive at the location, look at the base of the mountain, and you'll find a pit where a skeleton of a giant is buried . If you approach the skeleton, you'll spot the Darkbringer sword stuck in its jaw that you can pull. Thankfully, there aren't any enemies around, so you'll be able to freely walk away with the Darkbringer sword in your inventory.

Darkbringer in Crimson Desert is a tier four weapon that comes rolled with Critical Rate Lv. 3. However, the main attraction is the Ator's Orb Abyss Gear that activates when players perform heavy attacks (R2/RT/Right-Click) with the weapon. This shoots out multiple orbs of energy in the air that track enemies and damage them.

Subscribe to the newsletter for top Crimson Desert gear guides

Looking for deeper Crimson Desert weapon insight? Subscribe to our newsletter for clear, actionable breakdowns of weapons, locations, Abyss Gear combos and build ideas, plus similar deep-dive coverage across other game mechanics.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

This ability makes the weapon extremely viable during siege/liberation activities, as it'll mow down hordes of enemies more quickly. However, you have an option to extract the Ator's Orb Abyss Gear from the Darkbringer and use it with your favorite weapon.

Pair more Abyss Gear abilities that activate on heavy attack presses (R2/RT/Right-Click), and you'll make the most formidable weapon in Crimson Desert .

Crimson Desert

item.title: Bethesda Game is Officially Shutting Down in June
item.description:
Bethesda has announced that The Elder Scrolls: Blades will be permanently shut down on June 30, 2026, and has already been delisted from storefronts. The mobile spin-off game was originally released for Android, iOS, and Nintendo Switch in May 2020 and featured classic first-person dungeon-crawling gameplay and a story following the titular Blades, who have become exiled after the Great War. Now, after six years of service, The Elder Scrolls: Blades will no longer be accessible to players after June.

The Elder Scrolls: Blades is just one of Bethesda's mobile games, with other titles like Fallout Shelter and The Elder Scrolls: Castles remaining active for the time being. Compared to the mostly positive reception of these other titles, The Elder Scrolls: Blades received largely negative reviews, garnering a score of 37 on OpenCritic. Part of these criticisms came from the game's monetization model, being a free-to-play game with microtransactions that many players felt encouraged pay-to-win strategies.

The Elder Scrolls: Blades is Shutting Down on June 30

An in-game message to The Elder Scrolls: Blades players announced plans to shutter the game's servers on June 30. Players have also found that The Elder Scrolls: Blades has already been delisted from storefronts like Google Play, Apple App Store, and Nintendo eShop in anticipation of this shutdown. For players who already have the game installed on their device, they can continue playing until the June 30 shutdown date, but the game can't be accessed by players looking to start it for the first time.

One upside of this imminent shutdown is that Bethesda is offering a heavy discount on all items offered in The Elder Scrolls: Blades ' in-game store. Items will cost only 1 Gem or 1 Sigil each, and all players will receive a bundle of these currencies so that they can access all the content The Elder Scrolls: Blades has to offer before the game is taken offline for good. While the news of this shutdown may be disappointing for fans, they can at least have free rein over the game's content for the next two months.

The Elder Scrolls: Blades' Shutdown Comes at a Stagnant Time for the Franchise

Elder Scrolls fans are currently in a state of limbo, with the next mainline game on the horizon, but little else to get excited about. Last year's release of The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion Remastered marked a significant entry in the long wait for the next mainline game's release, but few updates on The Elder Scrolls 6 's development after its initial announcement at E3 2018 have only piled on the anticipation. While Bethesda's Todd Howard has confirmed that the majority of the studio is now working on The Elder Scrolls 6 , the game is likely still several years away from release.

The Elder Scrolls: Blades has been serving as one of the options for fans to find refuge in during this prolonged wait, but that option is about to be taken off the table. Thankfully, there are still other options available to pass the time, such as the aforementioned Oblivion Remastered . For Nintendo Switch 2 owners, Skyrim just received a 60 FPS update on the console, making it the perfect time to dive back in for another playthrough. Mobile Elder Scrolls: Blades players can instead swap to playing The Elder Scrolls: Castles , where they can manage their kingdom, expand and customize their castle, and send subjects out to explore and collect valuable items from dungeons. While these games might not fill the exact void left by The Elder Scrolls: Blades , players can at least keep themselves occupied within the world of Tamriel until The Elder Scrolls 6 releases.

The Elder Scrolls: Blades

Follow
Followed
Action RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 37 /100 Critics Rec: 8%
Released
May 12, 2020
ESRB

item.title: The 8 Most Anticipated Base-Building Games Officially Coming In 2026, Ranked
item.description:
Base-building games are some of the most beloved and popular releases of the last few years. Players who enjoy the satisfying journey of a good DIY project can spend hours at a time on their virtual endeavors. Whether it's building a floating home in the middle of the ocean on RAFT or nourishing a kingdom from the ground up on Medieval Dynasty , it's fair to say base-building games are a staple in any player's library.

With high replayability and always room for improvement, base-building games allow full creative freedom, really putting the power in players' hands. This list covers some of the most anticipated releases coming in 2026, some with early access demos available for download right now!

8 JOIN US

Doomsday Is Near

Join Us
Sandbox
Survival
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Wolf Haus Games
Genre(s)
Sandbox, Survival
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by

JOIN US takes inspiration from Grindhouse cinema of the 1970s, offering a humorous and satirical look into the world of cults. Building your compound from the ground up, taking on the responsibility of leading yourself, or perhaps working together with 3 others, as it isn't really a cult without your friends involved.

Convince your followers that you are their only savior, crafting a unique belief system and living with the consequences. You will need followers, and no matter if civilians love or hate you, there is a chance they'll join. Anyone who joins can be controlled by you or set to be a worker, so you have more time to recruit new followers, but remember to keep them happy, or they won't stay around for long. Building efficient housing, growing enough food, and holding sermons are only a few of your responsibilities. Spread successful propaganda, and soon you'll have doctors, farmers, and maidens lining up at your door. In JOIN US , enemies are imminent; once the secret of your little cult gets out, government officials and locals will rally together to end you. But the afterlife isn't half bad; in fact, now justice seems a lot easier than before.

7 Frostrail

Everything Is A Matter Of Survival

Frostrail
Open-World
Survival
Crafting
Adventure
Exploration
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
FakeFish
Genre(s)
Open-World, Survival, Crafting, Adventure, Exploration
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by

Aboard an old steam train is the only place you find comfort in this post-apocalyptic frozen wasteland. Frostrail will have players taking refuge on a moving train, constantly upgrading and operating as you navigate through the land of the cursed. Everything you need to keep going will be on board, including crafting tables, storage, and the ability to build new carriages as demand grows. The more you carry, the heavier your load. If you want to keep moving fast enough to escape enemies on the ground, you might want to think about a bigger engine. Stopping at abandoned factories and homesteads, looting is essential for survival, but you aren't alone out there, so come prepared with weapons. The cold isn't on your side either; don't wear too few layers and suffer a slow, suffocating fate.

On the way in 2026, Frostrail will offer early access in due time, involving player feedback in game improvements before official release. This adventure can be completed solo or joined by friends , and is perfect for those players who enjoy a little chaos in their games.

GameRant Quiz

—

QUESTION 1/1

Results

0

—

More Games
GameRant Quiz
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)
—
QUESTION 1/1
Results
0
—
More Games

6 SpaceCraft

The Universe Is Yours

SpaceCraft
Adventure
Simulation
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Shiro Games
Genre(s)
Adventure, Simulation
Platform(s)
Steam
Powered by

Actively accepting early access requests for at least 5 more months, SpaceCraft is a highly anticipated release coming in the second quarter of 2026 . Players can currently download the demo via Steam to try out this solo or co-op adventure-filled universe.

In a galaxy that evolves alongside player actions, teamwork will be your best friend. Spacecraft takes place in 2350, after humankind fled through a galactic portal to escape extinction. Exploring the vast web of planetary systems, looting abandoned space stations and ships, players will journey on their own custom-built spacecraft in search of survival. Extract resources to trade or use towards future builds, creating intricate bases on different planets with thought-out delivery routes for cargo drones and ships. Automating production is key for maximum efficiency, while defending yourself in combat is what matters if you plan on leaving your impact on the galaxy. If this sounds easy, then you’ll find yourself managing your very own crew in no time.

5 Permafrost

A Brutal White Hell

Permafrost
Survival
Crafting
Exploration
Action
Adventure
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Survival, Crafting, Exploration, Action, Adventure
Powered by

Awakened by frostbite, players can expect nothing less than a brutal frozen apocalypse when playing Permafrost , coming soon to Steam early access in 2026. Offering both solo and co-op play for groups of up to 4, players will have to build trusting bonds with one another to survive the relentless cold.

Surrounded by endless winter, you will battle hunger, exhaustion, and extreme weather, stopping at nothing to find the last connection to what you have lost. Pushing your survival skills to the limit, you will have to explore cold zones to find valuable loot and resources, with the help of your furry companion carrying a part of your load. You will encounter other survivors, but think fast; resources are scarce, and people are desperate. A successful trip back to your base after a raid makes crafting, upgrading, and restoring technology all the easier. Don't forget to hunt what remains of the wildlife; unfortunately, it's your only source of food for now.

4 Bloody Hell Hotel

Hospitality Is Not Your Strong Suit

Bloody Hell Hotel
Simulation
Base Building
Management
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Unfold Games
Genre(s)
Simulation, Base Building, Management
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by

Players who love games like House Flipper 2 and Supermarket Together will find endless fun in Bloody Hell Hotel . You will play as a vampire who has just awoken from a centuries-long coma, and it's safe to say, your now neglected living space could use some TLC. Renovating is your top priority, and thanks to your vampiric powers, the work will be a breeze. Monitor food and building resources, which you will harvest and farm in the basement. And if you are looking to save a little money, you can take care of those extra demanding guests by sucking their blood to refill your powers and consuming their corpses as food.

Bloody Hell Hotel is a single-player adventure set to release later this year in 2026. Developers are consistently releasing update announcements, which can be viewed on Steam. The most recent being the addition of trains on February 2nd, 2026.

3 Chronicles: Medieval

Glory and Legacy Is Yours

Chronicles: Medieval
Action
Strategy
RPG
Sandbox
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Action, Strategy, RPG, Sandbox
Powered by

Looking for ultra-realistic weapons, battles, and death? Look no further, Chronicles: Medieval will transport you right back to Medieval Europe, except this time it's your playground. Every decision matters, navigating conflicts among those who believe they are better and more deserving of royalty. You will start with nothing and earn your worth by showcasing your bravery and strength in large-scale battles and by how well you defend the castles you built from the ground up. Each story is unique. Are you motivated by fortune, power, or adventure? The more you fight, the more you learn. Leveling up, building, and adapting your tactics is key to success.

Priding themselves on evolving the game based on player feedback provided through Steam, the developers plan to release Chronicles: Medieval sometime in 2026 , with early access coming soon. Offering both solo and online co-op play experiences, players who enjoy some action and being kept on their toes are eagerly looking forward to release day.

2 Solarpunk

An Indie Airship Adventure

Solarpunk
Open-World
Survival
Base Building
Systems
Released
2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Open-World, Survival, Base Building
Powered by

Based in a world of floating islands, Solarpunk is planned to be released sometime in 2026, revealing exclusive updates about the game at The Triple-i Initiative on April 9th, 2026 , which can be streamed on YouTube or Twitch. Playing solo or with friends, Solarpunk offers a collaborative indie adventure that's not been seen before.

Subscribe for curated previews of 2026 base-building games

Our newsletter delivers curated coverage of base-building games—early-access demos, developer updates, and focused picks for 2026 releases. Get clear comparisons and practical context to decide which worlds and builds deserve your time.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

In this world, there is no exploitation, only coexistence, a vegan's dream! Animals on your land will reward you with their help, but only if treated well. Fruits and vegetables will be your main source of food. Don't expect a pig to find your truffles if you keep him locked up in a dark box. A well-thought-out farm will utilize automation, removing manual labor and leaving more time for exploring on your personal airship. Visiting new islands is key to discovering new resources (and maybe even a friend or 2), to expand and build further. With ever-changing weather conditions, utilizing different fuel sources throughout the seasons is vital.

1 Outbound

Traversal Sustainability

Outbound
Open-World
Survival
Crafting
Exploration
Systems
Released
April 23, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Everyone / Users Interact, In-Game Purchases
Genre(s)
Open-World, Survival, Crafting, Exploration
Powered by

Traveling far across different biomes in your electric campervan, either solo or with up to 3 other friends, Outbound is a colorful adventure players won't want to miss. Your van is your home, and as you progress, you will build out your campervan into something fabulous! Choosing to power your mobile home by either wind, sun, or water, you can explore landmarks and research new technology at your own pace. Build custom gardens to grow your own food, like blueberries and mushrooms, and perhaps if you share with the wildlife, they may reward you with something special. Really make it your own, customizing decorations, furniture, and paintings. Whether you live for the fresh air or prefer a more cozy living space, the possibilities are endless.

You can even add a furry friend to your adventures if you stop by the Paws & Whiskers Lodge.

This game, set in a near-utopian future, is great for players who love a casual, indie simulation game. Its bright colors and unique art style will be available to experience on the planned release date of April 23rd, 2026 . A demo is also currently available for download on Steam for players who are eager to give it a shot.

item.title: Magic: the Gathering May Be Bringing Back One of Its Most Powerful Cards Ever
item.description:
A new leak from Magic: The Gathering 's Secrets of Strixhaven expansion suggests that the game is bringing back one of its most powerful cards in a new way. The beloved TCG from Wizards of the Coast has seen an exciting start to the year, with multiple new sets already released to kick off 2026. January saw Magic: the Gathering return to one of the game's most popular planes with the release of Lorwyn Eclipsed, while Wizards of the Coast welcomed its newest crossover in March with Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles . The card game is also planning another big return to a fan-favorite setting.

Magic: The Gathering will officially debut Secrets of Strixhaven as its second in-universe set of the year in late April. The expansion whisks players back to the magical school of Strixhaven in the plane of Arcavios, said to explore more of the world outside the titular school. Wizards of the Coast has already given some early teasers showcasing multiple powerful cards in the main set and the Commanders for its pre-constructed decks. However, one new card may come as a big surprise to both old and new fans.

Magic: The Gathering Leaks Powerful New Strixhaven Card

A recent Secrets of Strixhaven leak introduced players to a brand-new creature, Emeritus of Ideation. The powerful Mythic creature card costs two Blue and three generic mana to cast for a flying creature with Ward 2 and an ability that causes it to enter "prepared," a new mechanic in Secrets of Strixhaven. The card's framing suggests that, when prepared, players will be able to use Emeritus of Ideation to cast Ancestral Recall, causing a targeted player to draw three cards. Ancestral Recall was originally featured as a member of Magic: The Gathering 's "Power Nine," a group of cards printed in the Alpha, Beta, and Unlimited sets that are widely accepted as some of the best cards in Magic 's history.

While Emeritus of Ideation's effect is clearly powerful, the card's inclusion of an Ancestral Recall-like effect has raised some big questions among the community. Beyond its status as a member of the Power Nine, Ancestral Recall is part of the Reserved List, a group of select cards that Wizards of the Coast has stated will never be reprinted. The return of Ancestral Recall with the prepared mechanic could open the door to new versions of powerful older cards being printed, potentially featuring even more Reserved List references in Secrets of Strixhaven. Wizards of the Coast has not made an official statement about the leak, planning its full Strixhaven reveal for March 31.

Emeritus of Ideation

  • Creature - Human Wizard
  • {U}{U}{3}
  • 5/5
  • Flying, Ward {2}
  • This creature enters prepared. When this creature attacks, you may exile eight cards from your graveyard. If you do, this creature becomes prepared.

Ancestral Recall (Prepared)

  • Instant
  • {U}
  • Target player draws three cards.

While the Reserved List has largely remained sacred, Wizards of the Coast has looked for ways to work around the policy. The 30th Anniversary Edition set featured reprints of numerous cards from early Magic sets, though the included cards were not tournament-legal due to their unique back. Head Magic: the Gathering designer Mark Rosewater has also stated that the existence of the Reserved List does not prevent Wizards of the Coast from printing different versions of cards on the list.

Secrets of Strixhaven's upcoming release is just one of several more sets coming throughout the remainder of 2026. Wizards of the Coast will follow up Secrets of Strixhaven with a new crossover set in June, adding new cards based on Marvel Super Heroes . Magic: The Gathering will add two more crossovers beyond Marvel , debuting collaborations with The Hobbit and Star Trek , while Reality Fracture will be the third and final in-universe set of the year. The card game has also continued to add new crossover cards through its frequent Secret Lair drops. Magic fans should have lots to look forward to from the game in the near future.

item.title: Genshin Impact Reveals All Banner Characters for Version 6.5
item.description:
Genshin Impact has officially unveiled its Chronicled and Event Banners for Version 6.5, as players will be able to pull for a total of nine different 5-Star characters over the course of the update. From a content standpoint, Version 6.5 focuses on two separate threads in Genshin Impact : the map expansion in Mondstadt that finally adds Dornman Port to the game, and the Flagship Event in Nod-Krai that summons characters from other nations to participate in a trade expo. While players will have to wait until Version 6.6 for Nicole and Lohen to further expand the ranks of the Hexerei, Version 6.5 comes with a few enticing reruns.

The first worth mentioning is Lauma and Nefer in Genshin Impact , a duo that takes the Lunar-Bloom reaction and catapults it to the top of the current meta. Players will be able to obtain both 5-Stars during Version 6.5, and to that end, HoYoverse has been kind enough to place Lauma and Nefer in the second half of the patch with enough time to stock up on Primogems by exploring Dornman Port and the Temple of Space. However, the real surprise of Version 6.5 lies elsewhere.

Genshin Impact: All 5-Star Banner Characters in Version 6.5

Name

Rarity

Element

Weapon

Debut

Linnea

5-Star

Geo

Bow

Version 6.5

Chasca

5-Star

Anemo

Bow

Version 5.2

Lauma

5-Star

Dendro

Catalyst

Version 6.0

Nefer

5-Star

Dendro

Catalyst

Version 6.1

Emilie

5-Star

Dendro

Polearm

Version 4.8

Clorinde

5-Star

Electro

Sword

Version 4.7

Navia

5-Star

Geo

Claymore

Version 4.3

Sigewinne

5-Star

Hydro

Bow

Version 4.7

Lyney

5-Star

Pyro

Bow

Version 4.0

As confirmed by the official Genshin Impact account on Reddit, Version 6.5 will feature both the usual duo of Event Banners, but also a Chronicled Banner for Fontaine characters. The first half will be all about Linnea in Genshin Impact , the new 5-Star Geo Bow whose ability kit can best be described as Lunar-Crystallize's answer to Lauma. She will be joined by Chasca, one of Natlan's most versatile hypercarries. The second half of Version 6.5, on the other hand, will be headlined by Lauma and Nefer, but it will also debut the Fontaine Chronicled Banner, featuring Navia, Clorinde, Emilie, Lyney, and Sigewinne.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

Like other Chronicled Banners in Genshin Impact , players will also be able to wish for Signature Weapons associated with the featured 5-Star characters. A few weapons worth mentioning include Verdict, Absolution, and The First Great Magic – all decent, universal options. Curiously, the Chronicled Banner is missing Wriothesley, a Fontaine character that is neither too recent (like Escoffier), nor particularly meta-friendly (like Neuvillette, Furina, and Arlecchino). The implications of this should be carefully examined.

Namely, it implies HoYoverse is preparing a separate rerun for Wriothesley in Genshin Impact in the near future, with Version 6.7 being the most likely candidate. With Sandrone rumored to be a Superconduct character set to make her playable debut in Version 6.7, Cryo may be undergoing another revival (beyond Freeze) in time for Snezhnaya. Whatever the case ends up being, many Genshin Impact players have found his absence either frustrating, surprising, or a mixture of both. Given the 5-Star waiting list for Genshin Impact , it is all but confirmed that the Liyue Chronicled Banner will shortly follow, as many of the region's characters are long overdue for getting a rerun. The biggest offenders in this regard include Hu Tao, Ganyu, Tartaglia, Xiao, and Baizhu, all of whom haven't been on any banner in well over a year.

For those concerned with the meta, Version 6.5 has two distinct paths for players to take: if they have a Geo hypercarry such as Navia or Zibai in Genshin Impact , they benefit the most from pulling for Linnea. If they lack a Lunar Reaction hypercarry entirely, then Nefer and Lauma may be worth the investment over anything else. Version 6.5 will last from April 8, 2026, all the way to May 20, 2026. This gives players ample time to make prudent decisions on how to spend their Primogems, as Version 6.6 will bring the focus back to the Hexerei.

8 /10

Genshin Impact

Follow
Followed
RPG
Action
Adventure
Gacha
Open-World
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 81 /100 Critics Rec: 87%
Released
September 28, 2020
ESRB
Teen / Alcohol Reference, Fantasy Violence, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Developer(s)
HoYoverse (Formerly miHoYo)
Publisher(s)
HoYoverse (Formerly miHoYo)
6 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

Step into Teyvat, a vast world teeming with life and flowing with elemental energy.

You and your sibling arrived here from another world. Separated by an unknown god, stripped of your powers, and cast into a deep slumber, you now awake to a world very different from when you first arrived.

Thus begins your journey across Teyvat to seek answers from The Seven — the gods of each element. Along the way, prepare to explore every inch of this wondrous world, join forces with a diverse range of characters, and unravel the countless mysteries that Teyvat holds...

MASSIVE OPEN WORLD
Climb any mountain, swim across any river, and glide over the world below, taking in the jaw-dropping scenery each step of the way. And if you stop to investigate a wandering Seelie or strange mechanism, who knows what you might discover?

ELEMENTAL COMBAT SYSTEM
Harness the seven elements to unleash elemental reactions. Anemo, Electro, Hydro, Pyro, Cryo, Dendro, and Geo interact in all sorts of ways, and Vision wielders have the power to turn this to their advantage.

Will you vaporize Hydro with Pyro, electro-charge it with Electro, or freeze it with Cryo? Your mastery of the elements will give you the upper hand in battle and exploration.

BEAUTIFUL VISUALS
Feast your eyes on the world around you, with a stunning art style, real-time rendering, and finely tuned character animations delivering you a truly immersive visual experience. Lighting and weather all change naturally over time, bringing every detail of this world to life.

SOOTHING SOUNDTRACK
Let the beautiful sounds of Teyvat draw you in as you explore the expansive world around you. Performed by the London Philharmonic Orchestra, the soundtrack changes seamlessly with the time and gameplay to match the mood.

BUILD YOUR DREAM TEAM
Team up with a diverse cast of characters in Teyvat, each with their own unique personalities, stories, and abilities. Discover your favorite party combinations and level up your characters to help you conquer even the most daunting of enemies and domains.

JOURNEY WITH FRIENDS
Team up with friends across various platforms to trigger more elemental action, tackle tricky boss fights, and conquer challenging domains together to reap rich rewards.

As you stand atop the peaks of Jueyun Karst and take in the rolling clouds and vast terrain stretching out before you, you may wish to stay in Teyvat a little longer... But until you are reunited with your lost sibling, how can you rest? Go forth, Traveler, and begin your adventure!

Genre(s)
RPG, Action, Adventure, Gacha, Open-World
Powered by
